title: A Song of Love.
content:
"Hey, rose, just born
    Twin to a thorn;
    Was't so with you, O Love and Scorn?

    "Sweet eyes that smiled,
    Now wet and wild;
    O Eye and Tear - mother and child.

    "Well: Love and Pain
    Be kinsfolk twain:
    Yet would, Oh would I could love again."
meaning:
Sidney Lanier's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Song of Love."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
